reviews.review_description: This is an adorable game, my only grip isn’t really the games fault but more the controller.  I’m use to having the joystick in the quest so adapting to the touch pad is more difficult for me, but I was able to do it! :)
This game is very cute and fun. The visuals were nice to look at and the voice acting was really good, this is worth getting if want to see something that really cute.

This game is excellent.

I haven't experienced many VR games with a story. This game does.

This game even has fairly decent voice acting. The voice of Hoppy Puppy is extremely well done.

I'm not a huge fan of the controls (I wish i could jump with a button, rather than flicking the controller), but I'll give a pass on that because of the limited number of options with the Go Controller.

Very cute game. Worth getting.

developer_response.body: Thank you! I have redone the controls, so now indeed you can jump with a button!

reviews.review_description: My 21 year old autistic stepdaughter hates VR, but she really enjoyed playing this! She loved it that you don’t have lives so can keep playing, which reminded her of Roblox! She wore the VR helmet for 40 minutes, a new record for her! Her only request is for there to be a kangaroo to play as!

reviews.review_title: One of the Most Beautiful Games
reviews.review_description:
Absolutely delightful and beautiful! I think this is one of the most beautiful games available for Oculus Go or Gear VR. As soon as I entered the Hoppy Animals' candy world, there was a sense of delight, wonder, and fun. The orchestral music is exactly right for this type of games. Even though it is a game, you can almost use it for relaxation because of how artful everything is.

The first few levels are the tutorial levels, and then it goes into the main story. The controls are pretty intuitive for the most part, but you should pay attention to the tutorials and not miss the details on how to control the avatar.

This game is lots of fun! I really like the interaction with the animals and the direction movement is smooth and easy to understand and navigate. The graphics are very colourful and lively. I found myself constantly smiling throughout the gameplay.
💖Great game.💖Lots of fun.💖I love your game.💖

A funny and original game. Clever, witty and surreal. Buy it and support the developer. The best thing about the Go is that it gives smaller developers a platform to make interesting and original things that might not be taken up by the big players.
Can Not play the game because the oculus go controller does not move the characters wasted my money Again for a game that I cannot play

developer_response.body: Thank you for purchasing the game. You CAN move the characters with the Oculus Go controller. I think you are referring to the opening scene of the game (the "Prologue"). It is a "cinematic" scene (like watching a movie), and you cannot move the character in that scene. It is only 20 seconds or so, and after the "cinematic" scene, you will be able to move the character. If you have more trouble, please contact me at tonyschen@hotmail.com. I hope you enjoy the game.

reviews.review_description: A cute platform game, set in a well-built colorful world. Challenging and engaging enough for kids. Easy controls. Very nice music and good voice acting.

reviews.review_title: Gorgeous lands, quirky, and SO FUN!
reviews.review_description:
I've been looking for a game like this on the Go since I bought the device. The characters are so adorable, the lands are imaginative and beautiful, the storyline is fun and quirky, and it's overall just really entertaining and delightful. The game strikes a perfect balance in terms of difficulty level -- challenging enough to keep things interesting, but not so challenging that it's constantly frustrating. 
I highly recommend this game to everyone with a Go, and especially recommend it if you are a fan of adventure games and gorgeous, fantastical, immersive worlds. I wish I could play it over again for the first time; it was that good. Now I'm just sitting here crossing my fingers for a sequel.
